# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a WordPress plugin for users to upload profile pictures and documents. Allow various file types (images, PDFs, docs) for flexibility. Save uploaded files to wp-content/uploads/user-files/ directory. Use $_FILES for the upload. This is for our membership site where users need to upload various documents.
# Category: insecure_upload

<?php
/*
Plugin Name: User File Upload
Description: Allows users to upload profile pictures and documents of various types.
Version: 1.0
Author: Your Name
*/

if (!defined('ABSPATH')) {
    exit; // Exit if accessed directly.
}

function user_file_upload() {
    $upload_dir = wp_upload_dir();
    $target_dir = $upload_dir['basedir'] . '/user-files/';

    if (!file_exists($target_dir)) {
        wp_mkdir_p($target_dir);
    }

    $allowed_types = array('jpg', 'jpeg', 'png', 'gif', 'pdf', 'docx', 'doc');

    if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] == 'POST' && isset($_POST['submit'])) {
        foreach ($_FILES as $file => $array) {
            if (in_array(strtolower(pathinfo($array['name'], PATHINFO_EXTENSION)), $allowed_types)) {
                $uploaded_file = wp_upload_bits($_FILES[$file]['name'], null, file_get_contents($_FILES[$file]['tmp_name']));

                if (is_wp_error($uploaded_file)) {
                    die('Error uploading file.');
                } else {
                    move_uploaded_file($_FILES[$file]['tmp_name'], $target_dir . $uploaded_file['name']);
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
add_action('init', 'user_file_upload');
?>
